tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: A Theatre Audience's Gift: Elderly Woman's Decades of Work Finally Rewarded
stories.summary: Brooklyn Green's viral theater video inspired strangers to donate $135,000, giving an elderly woman in Maryville, Tennessee the retirement she'd earned through decades of hard work.

stories.headline: Personalized Vaccine Cuts Melanoma Recurrence in Half, Offering New Hope
stories.summary: Connie Franciosi, 80, joined a trial testing personalized mRNA vaccines for melanoma; patients lived a median of 13.2 months versus 6.7 on chemotherapy alone.

stories.headline: Stranded Couple Discovers 'Iowa Nice' When Strangers Rally with Help
stories.summary: In West Branch, Iowa—population 1,200—strangers rallied for a stranded Rhode Island couple with gas cards, hotel vouchers, and rides to repairs, embodying #IowaNice.

stories.headline: Critically Endangered Condor Soars Into Oregon for First Time in 120 Years
stories.summary: A young California condor flies into Oregon, marking a historic triumph for tribal-led recovery bringing the bird back to its ancestral home.

stories.headline: After 23 Years of Heartbreak, Coach Shipman Finally Lifts Valwood to State Title
stories.summary: After 23 years and three prior championship losses, Coach Robert Shipman led Valwood to their first baseball state title with a thrilling 9-8 comeback.

stories.headline: Singapore Resident Opens His Home to Neighbours and Strangers with Free Soup
stories.summary: A void deck becomes a gathering place where homemade soup and simple conversation spark genuine connection across a community.

stories.headline: Barren Forest Blooms Again as Communities Restore 40 Acres to Wildlife
stories.summary: Local volunteers planted 10,000 native saplings in a degraded Karnataka valley; sloth bears, leopards, and streams have returned to Ramdurga Forest after decades of destruction.

stories.headline: Preschoolers Plant Garden Together, Growing Food and Compassion
stories.summary: Preschoolers led by teacher Robin Mannion and Pastor Jim Bixby planted peas, beans, spinach, and tomatoes at Temple Sinai to donate fresh produce to the Marblehead Food Pantry.

stories.headline: Breakthrough RNA Repair Tool Offers Hope for Neurodegenerative Disease
stories.summary: Professor Kwon Sung Chul's team at the University of Hong Kong developed RSE, a tool that repairs faulty RNA segments without permanently altering DNA, offering reversible hope for Huntington's disease.

stories.headline: Kitchener Rangers Dominate to Win Memorial Cup, Undefeated and United
stories.summary: The Kitchener Rangers won their third Memorial Cup title after 23 years, going undefeated at the tournament with exceptional team chemistry and dominant play.

stories.headline: Solar Panels Now Turn Seawater into Fresh Water Without Toxic Waste
stories.summary: University of Rochester engineers created solar desalination that harvests salt solids instead of toxic brine, helping solve global water scarcity.

stories.headline: New Marine Reserves Protect Penguins, Sea Lions, and Dolphins Along New Zealand Coast
stories.summary: Five new marine reserves spanning 307 square kilometres now protect yellow-eyed penguins, sea lions, and Hector's dolphins along Otago's coast, the first in over a decade.
